en: Oh that's like asking me to choose between my children literally
ko: 아이들 중에 고르라는 것과 같아요. 말 그대로

en: So my job as a composer starts at something called a spotting session which is a meeting in which the
ko: 작곡가로서 제 일은 스팟팅 세션이라는 것에서 시작하는데 작곡가가

en: composer meets with the producers the directors the writers music supervisors and other members of the crew to
ko: 프로듀서, 감독, 작가, 음악 감독 및 다른 스태프와 만나서

en: watch an episode all the way through and talk about where music should go in any given scene whether there should be
ko: 에피소드를 처음부터 끝까지 보면서 어떤 장면에 음악이 들어갈지 안 들어갈지

en: music or shouldn't be music and whether that music should be composed or licensed and hopefully some insight into what it should sound like
ko: 작곡할지 라이선스할지, 어떤 소리가 나야 하는지 논의하는 자리예요

en: I don't think I'm ever confident enough to say that I nailed anything
ko: 뭔가를 완벽하게 해냈다고 자신할 만큼 확신한 적은 없어요

en: It's always a tricky thing scoring for TV and film but I can share an interesting story that
ko: TV와 영화 음악은 항상 까다롭지만 베터 콜 사울과 관련된

en: relates to Better Call Saul We were working on the last season and I wrote
ko: 흥미로운 이야기를 공유할게요. 마지막 시즌 작업 중이었는데

en: I was working on one of the later episodes that begins with Howard Hamlin's car left at the beach It's
ko: 후반부 에피소드 중 하나에서 하워드 햄린의 차가 해변에 놓여있는 장면으로 시작하는데

en: one of the last things I shot for the show and I wrote I was working through that episode sequentially and I wrote a piece of music for
ko: 마지막으로 촬영한 장면 중 하나였고 순서대로 작업하면서 도입부에 음악을 썼는데

en: the beginning of that and then as I and I liked it and I was moving through it there's a very
ko: 마음에 들었고 진행하다 보니 하워드 햄린을 매장하는 유명한 장면이 끝에 있었는데

en: famous scene at the end where they bury Howard Hamlin and I realized that the music I wrote for early in the episode would
ko: 도입부에 쓴 음악이 그쪽에 훨씬 잘 맞는다는 걸 깨달았어요

en: really actually work much better there and so we ended up moving it there and I just instinctively knew that that I
ko: 거기로 옮겼고 직감적으로 맞는 아이디어를 찾은 건데

en: had found the right idea only I had found it in the wrong spot
ko: 잘못된 위치에서 찾은 거였죠

en: So and I always love to take advantage of the fact that composers are last in the process so
ko: 작곡가가 제작 과정에서 마지막이라는 걸 항상 활용해요

en: I get to watch these shows like a fan They're basically built and done by the time I see them and so I get
ko: 팬처럼 볼 수 있거든요. 제가 볼 때쯤이면 거의 완성되어 있어서

en: to sit down and watch them and feel how I hope the audience feels and maybe hopefully instinctually that
ko: 앉아서 관객이 느꼈으면 하는 대로 느끼면서 직감적으로

en: helps me figure out where the music should go and what it should do
ko: 음악이 어디에 가야 하고 뭘 해야 하는지 파악하는 데 도움이 되죠

en: Oh that's like asking me to choose between my children literally
ko: 아이들 중에 고르라는 것과 같아요. 말 그대로

en: My children were born during these shows
ko: 이 프로그램들을 하는 동안 아이들이 태어났거든요

en: I don't think I could do that I really don't I mean on any given day if you woke me up I might feel differently
ko: 못 고르겠어요. 정말. 아무 날이나 깨우면 다르게 느낄 수도 있지만

en: I think only because it's more recent probably better hustle That would be my answer today
ko: 아마 최근이라서 베터 허슬이요. 오늘의 답은 그거예요

en: You know I just think it's so exciting It's the first time we're doing something that's outside of the original universe
ko: 정말 흥미로워요. 원래 세계관 밖의 것을 처음 하는 거니까

en: and it is totally new and totally different I think you'll see a lot of the qualities that you
ko: 완전히 새롭고 다른 거예요. 우리 쇼에서 기대하는 품질은 보이겠지만

en: expect from our shows but it's a whole new thing and we're super excited to share it with
ko: 완전 새로운 거고 여러분과 공유하게 되어 정말 설레요

en: everybody
ko: 트윈 픽스의 안젤로 바달라멘티를 고르겠어요. 차점은

ko: 최근 떠나신 훌륭한 마크 스노우, X파일 음악을 쓰신 분이요

en: the recently departed and wonderful Mark Snow who wrote the score for The X Files
ko: 제 음악에 잘 안 쓰지만 피아노로 작곡하고 아이디어를 얻어요

en: Even though I rarely feature them in my scores I often write at the piano and get ideas at the piano and
ko: 다섯 살부터 배운 거니까 피아노요

en: that's where I learned from age five so piano
ko: 스튜디오에서 음식은 절대 안 먹고 커피도 안 마셔요 믿기 어렵겠지만

en: No food ever in the studio however and I don't drink coffee either believe it or not but late
ko: 늦은 밤에 정말 필요하면 나가서

en: night if I really need it I'm out and do
